title: 5 Selecting a Wires Processing Model
body_text:
5	Selecting a Wires Processing Model
5.1	Overview of Wires Processing Models
We describe three models for processing wires in this Chapter, but there are other variations. Select the model that best suits your needs and modify as required.
Note:	Wires branch administration and user permissions, including limits, are managed in MTS.
Centralized Environment
芒聙聯 A designated branch (e.g., the head office) sends and receives wires on behalf of all branches. This model is suitable for multi-branch financial institutions with an administration centre.
Partially Centralized Environment
芒聙聯 The branches have access to some Wires functions (e.g., creating outgoing wires), but a designated branch processes wires on behalf of the branches in some regard (e.g., receiving incoming wires). This model is suitable for multi-branch financial institutions with an administration centre.
Decentralized MTS Environment
芒聙聯 Each branch sends and receives its own wires. This model is suitable for financial institutions with one branch. Multi-branch financial institutions can consider this model if their branches process wires independently of each other.
Central 1 recommends that a designated group (e.g., compliance) reviews and approves high-value wires as a best practice.
5.2	Setting up a Centralized Wires Environment
In a centralized Wires environment, a designated branch (e.g., the head office) creates and receives wires on behalf of all branches. This model is suitable for a multi-branch financial institution with a central administration centre.
Set up Wires Users, MTS Administrators, and branches as follows:
Wires Users
芒聙聯
A minimum of three users are required. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS.
芒聢聮
Set up all users under one home transit number (e.g., the head office).
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Use Linked Transits芒聙聺 permission.
芒聢聮
Assign all Inbox permissions. This allows users to receive incoming wires on behalf of other branches.
芒聢聮
Assign the following Outbox permissions: 芒聙聹View Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺, 芒聙聹Create Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺, and 芒聙聹Authorize Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺 to each user. This allows users to create and authorize outgoing wires on behalf of other branches.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Permanently Delete Templates芒聙聺 permission to users authorized to manage wire templates that are pending deletion. See Section 18.7, Permanently Deleting a Wire Template, and Section 18.6, Restoring a Wire Template, for more information.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Spreads and Fees芒聙聺 permission to users authorized to manage the FX spreads and outgoing wire fees. See Chapter 12, Managing Foreign Exchange Spreads in Wires, and Chapter 11, Managing Outgoing Wire Fees.
Note:	Users require a 2-Step security token to receive and approve wires. At least one authorized user must approve all outgoing wires. See Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires.
MTS Administrators
芒聙聯 A minimum of two MTS Administrators are required. See Section 7.5, Creating an MTS Administrator.
芒聢聮
Set up all administrators under the same home transit number as the users.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Use Linked Transits芒聙聺 permission to each administrator and assign other administrative permissions as required.
Branches
芒聙聯 The branch settings, except for linking branches, affect outgoing wires only. See Section 9.5, Entering Branch Settings in MTS.
5.3	Setting up a Partially Centralized Wires Environment
In a partially centralized Wires environment, a designated branch (e.g., the head office) processes wires on behalf of the branches in some capacity (e.g., receives incoming wires). The branches have access to certain Wires functions (e.g., creating outgoing wires). This model is suitable for multi-branch financial institutions with an administration centre.
A partially centralized Wires environment can be set up in several ways. This section provides an example where the head office receives incoming wires for all branches, but each branch creates outgoing wires.
Set up Wires Users, administrators, and branches as follows:
Wires Users
芒聙聯 In this example, users from the head office receive incoming wires on behalf of all branches, and users from each branch create outgoing wires. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS, for procedures.
芒聢聮
Incoming Wire Users 芒聙聯 A minimum of one user is required. Create these users under a designated home transit number (e.g., the head office) and assign them the 芒聙聹Inbox芒聙聺 and 芒聙聹Use Linked Transits芒聙聺 permissions only.
Note:	We recommended that multiple users have access to this function to account for holidays and absences.
芒聢聮
Outgoing Wire Users
芒聙聯
Create a minimum of three users for each branch and assign these users the following Outbox permissions: 芒聙聹View Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺, 芒聙聹Create Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺, and 芒聙聹Authorize Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺. A least one authorized user must approve all outgoing wires. See Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Permanently Delete Templates芒聙聺 permission to users authorized to manage wire templates that are pending deletion. See Section 18.7, Permanently Deleting a Wire Template, and Section 18.6, Restoring a Wire Template, for more information.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Spreads and Fees芒聙聺 permission to users authorized to manage the FX spreads and outgoing wire fees. See Chapter 12, Managing Foreign Exchange Spreads in Wires, and Chapter 11, Managing Outgoing Wire Fees.
MTS Administrators
芒聙聯 A minimum of two MTS Administrators are required. In this example, the Administrators can manage users from all branches. See Section 7.5, Creating an MTS Administrator, for procedures.
芒聢聮
Set up the administrators under the same home transit number as the users assigned Incoming Transfers permissions
.
This isn芒聙聶t mandatory, but it芒聙聶s easier to link branches if these users and the administrators are set up under the same home transit number.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Use Linked Transits芒聙聺 permission to each administrator and assign other administrative permissions as required.
Branches
芒聙聯 The branch settings, except for linking branches, affect outgoing wires only. See Section 9.5, Entering Branch Settings in MTS.
芒聢聮
Enter the Level 1 Authorization and Level 2 Authorization limits for each branch. Ensure that the authorization limits are entered before the first outgoing wire is created. The settings are critical.
芒聢聮
Open the branch settings of the home transit number to which the administrators and users (those assigned Incoming Transfer permissions) are assigned. Link all other branches to that transit number. This allows the administrators, and the users assigned incoming wire permissions to potentially access all branches (individuals must also be assigned access to the Use Linked Transits permission).
5.4	Setting up a Decentralized Wires Environment
In a decentralized Wires environment, each branch creates and receives its own wires. Each branch can also manage its own users and administrators. This model is suitable for financial institutions with one branch. Multi-branch financial institutions can use this model if each branch processes its own incoming and outgoing wires.
Set up MTS Users, administrators, and branches as follows:
Wires Users
芒聙聯 A minimum of three users pe
r branch
is required. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS.
芒聢聮
Set up users for each branch under the transit number for each branch.
芒聢聮
Assign the appropriate Inbox and Outbox permissions to each user. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS, for procedures. The 芒聙聹Use Linked Transits芒聙聺 permission isn芒聙聶t required. At least one authorized user must approve all outgoing wires. See Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Permanently Delete Templates芒聙聺 permission to users authorized to manage wire templates that are pending deletion. See Section 18.7, Permanently Deleting a Wire Template, and Section 18.6, Restoring a Wire Template, for more information.
芒聢聮
Assign the 芒聙聹Spreads and Fees芒聙聺 permission to users authorized to manage the FX spreads and outgoing wire fees. See Chapter 12, Managing Foreign Exchange Spreads in Wires, and Chapter 11, Managing Outgoing Wire Fees.
MTS Administrators
芒聙聯 A minimum of two MTS Administrators per branch is required. See Section 7.5, Creating an MTS Administrator.
芒聢聮
Set up administrators for each branch under the transit number for each branch.
芒聢聮
Assign the appropriate permissions to each administrator. The 芒聙聹Use Linked Transits芒聙聺 and 芒聙聹Linked Transit Assignment芒聙聺 permissions aren芒聙聶t required.
Branches
芒聙聯 In a decentralized environment, branches aren芒聙聶t linked.
Enter the Level 1 Authorization limit and the Level 2 Authorization limit for each branch. Ensure that these limits are entered before the first outgoing wire is created. The settings are critical.
Important
:	Don芒聙聶t link any branches.
END
